hero.subtitle: Ophthalmology Practice
hero.title: Exceptional eye care
hero.description: Experience personalized ophthalmology services designed for your unique vision needs

comprehensive.title: Comprehensive Eye Examination
comprehensive.intro: Full eye health assessment using advanced diagnostic technologies.
comprehensive.diagnosticsList:
• — Visual acuity testing (visometry)
  • — Autorefractometry
  • — Ophthalmoscopy
  • — Keratometry
  • — Glasses prescription: monofocal, bifocal, and progressive
  • — Soft contact lens fitting (spherical and toric)
comprehensive.treatmentList:
• — Conjunctivitis
  • — Dry eye syndrome
  • — Accommodation spasm
  • — Asthenopia and other functional vision disorders

visometry.title: Visual Acuity Testing (Visometry)
visometry.description: Checks how well you see at distance and near using Sivtsev, Golovin, and Landolt charts or projection systems. Conducted with and without correction (with/without glasses) to determine your precise vision level.

autorefractometry.title: Objective Refraction Determination (Autorefractometry)
autorefractometry.description: Uses an autorefractometer to measure your eye's refractive power objectively. Determines the presence of myopia (nearsightedness), hyperopia (farsightedness), or astigmatism with precision.

subjective.title: Subjective Refractometry (Glasses Prescription)
subjective.description: Selection of optical lenses for maximum visual acuity using trial frames with lens sets. Our optometrist determines which diopter provides the best vision correction tailored to your unique needs.

accommodation.title: Accommodation Assessment
accommodation.description: Examines your eye's ability to focus at different distances. Determines accommodation reserve and stability, especially important for diagnosing eye strain, accommodation spasm, and focusing problems.

keratometry.title: Keratometry
keratometry.description: Measures corneal curvature with precision. Essential for contact lens fitting and helps detect astigmatism or keratoconus. Provides critical data for accurate vision correction.

ophthalmoscopy.title: Fundus Examination (Ophthalmoscopy)
ophthalmoscopy.description: Determines the condition of your retina, optic nerve (optic disc), retinal vessels, and macula (yellow spot). Helps detect glaucoma, optic nerve edema or atrophy, diabetic and hypertensive retinopathy, retinal detachment, inflammation, and fundus tumors.

prescriptionGlasses.title: Prescription Glasses
prescriptionGlasses.description: Custom eyewear adapted to your vision needs and style. Wide selection of frame styles, lens types (monofocal, progressive, bifocal), and specialized coatings including anti-reflective, blue light blocking, and UV protection. Professional fitting by Dr. Karimova for all ages.

contactsAndTreatment.title: Contact Lenses & Treatment
contactsAndTreatment.description: Professional fitting and care: daily disposable, monthly replacement, toric lenses for astigmatism, multifocal lenses. Treatment and patient management for conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, accommodation spasm, and visual fatigue. Prevention for high visual load (computer work, driving). Complete training and medication management.

dryEye.title: Dry Eye Management
dryEye.description: Assessment and treatment of dry eye syndrome: tear film evaluation, identifying the cause (screen work, contact lens wear, climate, medication), and a personalised therapy plan — lubricating drops, lid hygiene and habit changes. A follow-up visit confirms the treatment is working.

ongoingCare.title: Ongoing Care and Follow-Up
ongoingCare.description: Continuous monitoring for chronic and progressive conditions — glaucoma, diabetic and hypertensive retinopathy, progressive myopia, accommodation disorders. Scheduled repeat examinations track how your eyes change over time, so glasses, lenses or therapy can be adjusted before problems develop.

faq.subtitle: Common Questions
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Find answers to common questions about our eye care services in Dushanbe
faq.question1: How often should I get an eye exam?
faq.answer1: Adults with no vision problems should get comprehensive eye exams every 2 years. If you wear glasses, have diabetes, or family history of eye disease, annual exams are recommended. Children need exams at 6 months, age 3, before school, then annually.
faq.question2: Do you see children? At what age?
faq.answer2: Yes! Dr. Karimova has specialized training in pediatric ophthalmology and welcomes children from infancy through teenage years using child-friendly testing methods.
faq.question3: What is dry eye syndrome and how is it treated?
faq.answer3: Dry eye syndrome causes burning, itching, redness, and watery eyes due to insufficient tear production or poor tear quality. Treatment includes prescription eye drops, punctal plugs, warm compress therapy, and lifestyle modifications. Most patients see improvement within 2-4 weeks.
faq.question4: How long does a comprehensive eye exam take?
faq.answer4: A comprehensive eye examination takes approximately 45-60 minutes for your first visit. This includes visual acuity testing, refraction assessment, intraocular pressure measurement, retinal health evaluation, and consultation with Dr. Karimova.
faq.answer5: Yes, we provide professional contact lens fitting including measurements of eye curvature and tear film quality. We offer daily disposable, monthly replacement, toric lenses for astigmatism, and multifocal lenses. Complete training and ongoing support are included.
faq.question6: Where is your clinic located in Dushanbe?
faq.answer6: Our clinic is located inside Osse Optical Store at Bekhzod Street 14, Dushanbe, Tajikistan. After your appointment you can fill your prescription glasses same-day at Osse. Flexible appointment times including same-day urgent care for eye emergencies. Contact us at +992 108 11 80 80.
faq.cta: Still have questions? We're here to help.

privacy.metaDescription: How sitorakarimi.com handles data: what is collected, which analytics and third-party services are used, and how to reach us.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This website is operated by the ophthalmology practice of Dr. Sitora Karimova, located inside Osse Optical at Bekhzod Street 14, Dushanbe, Tajikistan. This page explains what information the site collects and how it is used. In short: there are no accounts, no booking forms, and no medical information is collected here.

sections.heading: Information we collect
sections.body:
This website does not ask you to create an account and has no contact or booking form. We do not collect your name, phone number, email address, or any medical information through this site.

When you visit, our analytics provider automatically records standard technical data: pages viewed, approximate location (city level), referring website, device type, browser and language. This data is aggregated and is not used to identify you personally.

sections.heading: Cookies and analytics
sections.body:
We use Google Analytics, loaded through Google Tag Manager, to understand which pages are useful to visitors. It stores cookies in your browser to recognise repeat visits. You can block these through your browser settings or a content blocker — the website works normally without them.

We do not use advertising or retargeting cookies.

sections.heading: Interactive vision tools
sections.body: The vision test and the vision disorder simulator run entirely inside your browser. Your answers and results are never sent to us and are never stored — they disappear as soon as you reload the page. These tools are educational and are not a medical diagnosis.

sections.heading: Third-party services
sections.body:
Parts of this site are provided by third parties, which may receive your IP address when their content loads:

  • Google Maps — the embedded clinic map.
  • Google Tag Manager / Google Analytics — usage statistics.
  • Cloudflare CDN — icon fonts.

Text fonts are served from our own server, so no font provider receives your data.

sections.heading: If you contact us
sections.body: If you call, message us on WhatsApp, or send an email, we receive what you send and use it only to reply to you and, where relevant, to arrange an appointment. Those conversations happen outside this website and are covered by the terms of the service you used.

sections.heading: Your rights and how to reach us
sections.body:
You may ask what data we hold about you, ask us to delete it, or object to analytics tracking. Write to info@sitorakarimi.com or call +992 108 11 80 80.

If this policy changes, the date at the top of this page will be updated.
